fact: Previously, Ms. Zhan was a portfolio manager and managing director at GMO, a Boston-based global investment leader (AUM $68B), from 2002 to 2011, where she was recognized for the firm's successful Greater China equity research and emerging market portfolio management. (Source: beyondgm.com/about/, confirmed live as of September 2025 per search index date.)

With Three-Year Revenue Growth of 192 Percent, Beyond Global Management Ranks No. 2,651 on the 2024 Inc. 5000 and No. 161 in Financial Services Among America's Fastest-Growing Private Companies. (Source: BusinessWire press release, August 15, 2024; mirrored on beyond-wm.com company news page, still live as of July 2026.)

Beyond Global Management is an alternative investment management firm focused on private credit collateralized by real estate assets. Founded in 2020, the firm is led by an executive team with over 100 years of combined investment experience across market cycles.

Beyond Paradise 1 (Kainani Townhomes, Keauhou, Kailua-Kona, Big Island Hawaii) has received USCIS I-956F approval (confirmed February 13, 2026 per eb5heic.com), is actively under construction (site visit conducted February 18–22, 2026), has created over 100 jobs as of January 2026 (averaging 41 jobs per investor, well above the 10-job minimum), received its first I-526E petition approval in 7 months, and is structured with defined exit terms including a 3-year investment period, 1-year exit period, 1-year backup period, and a 6-month capital return window upon investor withdrawal or sustainability period completion.

Jenny Zhan, founder and CEO of Beyond Holding Company, is staking bold capital on distressed commercial real estate assets at a time when most institutional players are tightening their exposure. She describes the market as facing 'a 1-2-3 punch'—first the shock of the pandemic, then a surge in interest rates, and now skyrocketing construction costs—and is doubling down on distressed properties. (GlobeSt, August 18, 2025)

From 2001 to 2016, Mr. Ye worked for PwC China and Singapore as a managing director where he advised more than 50 Fortune 500 multi-nationals, large corporations and leading businesses operating across different markets. Prior to joining Beyond, he was a Director with Good Hope Investment from 2017 to 2019, where he led the business development effort and took part in capital raising and operations for the private fund business in the US market. (Source: beyondgm.com/about/, cached September 22, 2025; corroborated by ContactOut April 2025, RocketReach, Crunchbase, and AeroLeads referencing PwC Aug 2001–Nov 2016 and CreditEase/Good Hope 2017.)

Jason Ye posted on LinkedIn: 'I am representing Beyond Global Management to attend Global Alts Asia 2024 from November 11-13 at Marina Bay Sands, Singapore!' — corroborated by multiple third-party sources confirming the event took place at Marina Bay Sands, Singapore, November 11–13, 2024.
